name: Tour of the Alto Mijares — Circular from Onda
name_original: Vuelta por el Alto Mijares — Circular desde Onda
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Onda are characterized by their diverse natural landscapes, situated at the foot of the Sierra de Espadán Natural Park. The region features extensive cork oak forests, rugged mountainous terrain, and significant elevation gains, providing challenging routes for experienced cyclists. The area also includes the scenic Sichar Reservoir, offering varied terrain for different fitness levels. This combination of natural features makes Onda a notable destination for road cycling. Best road cycling routes around Onda The most popular road cycling route is Tour of the Alto Mijares — Circular from Onda, a 52.2 miles (83.9 km) trail that takes 3 hours 42 minutes to complete. This route features substantial elevation gain through mountainous terrain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Natural Park of Serra d'Espada — Circular from Onda, a difficult 47.0 miles (75.7 km) path. It traverses the Sierra de Espadán Natural Park, known for its cork oak forests and challenging climbs. Local road cyclists also love the Sichar Reservoir – Vía Augusta loop from Onda, a 30.0 miles (48.3 km) trail leading through the scenic Sichar Reservoir area, often completed in about 2 hours 6 minutes. Road cycling around Onda is defined by mountainous terrain, cork oak forests, and the scenic Sichar Reservoir. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to difficult routes with significant elevation. The routes in Onda are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 9 reviews. More than 2,300 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Onda's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Onda?
A: Onda offers a wide selection of road cycling routes, with over 60 different tours available. These routes cater to various fitness levels, ranging from moderate loops to challenging rides with significant elevation gains.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes near Onda?
A: Road cycling routes around Onda are characterized by diverse natural landscapes. You'll encounter extensive cork oak forests, rugged mountainous terrain, and the scenic Sichar Reservoir area. Many routes feature significant elevation gains, especially those venturing into the Sierra de Espadán Natural Park.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easier ride in Onda?
A: Yes, Onda provides options for cyclists seeking a more moderate experience. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/34007471" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sichar Reservoir – Vía Augusta loop from Onda&lt;/a&gt; offer a good balance of distance and elevation, leading through the picturesque Sichar Reservoir area. Another moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39548683" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sitjar Dam – Sichar Reservoir loop from Onda&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Where can I find challenging road cycling routes with significant elevation around Onda?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Onda is an excellent base. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e883326427/tour-of-the-alto-mijares-circular-from-onda" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour of the Alto Mijares — Circular from Onda&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding route with substantial elevation gain through mountainous terrain. Another difficult option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e883129354/natural-park-of-serra-d-espada-circular-from-onda" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Park of Serra d'Espada — Circular from Onda&lt;/a&gt;, which traverses the challenging Sierra de Espadán Natural Park. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17514003" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll d’Aín – Aín Village loop from Onda&lt;/a&gt; also presents a significant climb.

Q: What natural attractions or landmarks can I see while road cycling near Onda?
A: Many routes offer stunning views and pass by notable landmarks. You can cycle through the Sierra de Espadán Natural Park, known for its cork oak forests and rugged peaks. The scenic Sichar Reservoir is also a prominent feature. For historical sights, you might catch glimpses of the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2756864" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro Castle&lt;/a&gt; or the iconic Onda Castle, which dominates the town's skyline. Mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2472919" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Eslida Pass&lt;/a&gt; also offer panoramic vistas.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes starting from Onda?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Onda are designed as circular loops, making them convenient for starting and ending in the town. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e883326427/tour-of-the-alto-mijares-circular-from-onda" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour of the Alto Mijares — Circular from Onda&lt;/a&gt; and the more moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/34007471" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sichar Reservoir – Vía Augusta loop from Onda&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Onda?
A: The road cycling routes in Onda are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 9 reviews. Cyclists frequently praise the diverse terrain, the beauty of the Sierra de Espadán Natural Park, and the scenic views around the Sichar Reservoir. The variety of routes catering to different fitness levels is also a highlight.

Q: Is Onda a good destination for cycling tourism?
A: Absolutely. Onda's location at the foot of the Sierra de Espadán Natural Park, combined with a network of well-defined routes and diverse landscapes, makes it a prime location for cycling tourism. The region around Castellón, including Onda, is recognized for its cycling potential, offering routes that explore both natural beauty and charming villages.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints or scenic spots along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer excellent viewpoints, particularly those that climb into the mountainous areas. The Sierra de Espadán Natural Park itself provides spectacular scenery. You might also find panoramic views from higher points like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4760025" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Penya Migdia&lt;/a&gt; or from the vicinity of Onda Castle, which offers unique perspectives of the surrounding landscapes.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Onda?
A: While specific seasonal considerations are not detailed, the Mediterranean climate of the region generally makes spring and autumn ideal for road cycling, offering pleasant temperatures. Summer can be quite warm, especially in the middle of the day, while winter typically brings milder conditions compared to more northern regions, making it possible to cycle year-round.

Q: Are there any historical sites or cultural points of interest accessible by road bike from Onda?
A: Yes, road cycling routes can lead you to areas rich in history. Onda itself boasts the impressive 10th-century Onda Castle, also known as the 'Castle of 300 Towers.' The town's historic center, with its medieval corners and ceramic panels, is also worth exploring. The Molí de la Reixa, a former flour mill converted into a Nature Interpretation Center, offers insights into Onda's natural heritage.
tips: Demanding stretch if done uphill from Fanzara to Montanejos. You pass through the port of Vallat, one of the most colorful and picturesque of Alto Mijares. In addition to being colorful, this section is very quiet, practically without traffic and with pavement in excellent condition.

Water, walks and corners full of serenity await you in Montanejos, where you can visit natural fountains in the middle of nature.
Talking about the history of Montanejos is talking about its Black Cave, where we find ourselves in the Neolithic period, discovering that its first settlers were already seduced by this location next to the Mijares River.
